sections.heading: Surgery
sections.content: Surgery plays a critical and variable role across risk groups in neuroblastoma management. For low-risk disease, surgical resection may be curative. For intermediate-risk disease, surgery is used in combination with chemotherapy. For high-risk disease, surgical resection is performed after neoadjuvant chemotherapy as part of induction therapy [NEUROB-E 1 of 5].

cards.name: Open biopsy
cards.detail:
Approach: Incisional biopsy via open approach
Indications: Indicated when resection at diagnosis is not possible or not recommended. Generally indicated for patients with metastatic disease or L2 tumors. May be preferred in scenarios where minimally invasive techniques cannot obtain adequate tissue [NEUROB-E 1 of 5].
Key complications: Hemorrhage; tissue inadequacy if necrotic specimen obtained [NEUROB-E 4 of 5].
Operative mortality: Not specified in source.

cards.name: Laparoscopic/thoracoscopic biopsy
cards.detail:
Approach: Minimally invasive biopsy
Indications: Indicated when resection at diagnosis is not possible. Should result in collection of >1 cm³ of fresh viable tissue [NEUROB-E 1 of 5].
Key complications: Hemorrhage; tissue inadequacy; insufficient tissue for full biomarker profiling [NEUROB-E 1 of 5].
Operative mortality: Not specified in source.

cards.name: Core needle biopsy
cards.detail:
Approach: Percutaneous (surgeon or interventional radiologist)
Indications: Can be performed to obtain tissue. Should result in collection of at least 10 cores to ensure specimen adequacy for biomarker profiling [NEUROB-E 1 of 5].
Key complications: Inadequate tissue; hemorrhage [NEUROB-E 1 of 5].
Operative mortality: Not specified in source.

cards.name: Primary tumor resection (post-neoadjuvant chemotherapy)
cards.detail:
Approach: Open surgical resection (cervical, thoracic, abdominal, or paraspinal approaches depending on tumor location)
Indications: After neoadjuvant chemotherapy in intermediate- and high-risk disease. Goal is gross total resection [NEUROB-E 2 of 5].
Key complications: Hemorrhage (major risk with infiltrative tumors), nerve injuries, organ dysfunction if vital structures compromised [NEUROB-E 4 of 5]. Intraoperative complications are site-dependent.
Operative mortality: Not specified in source.

cards.name: Laparotomy and silo placement
cards.detail:
Approach: Open abdominal surgery
Indications: Extreme situations in infants with abdominal compartment syndrome, severe respiratory compromise, or life-threatening symptoms secondary to hepatomegaly [NEUROB-E 2 of 5].
Key complications: Intra-abdominal pressure complications; infection; organ dysfunction [NEUROB-E 2 of 5].
Operative mortality: Not specified in source.
